rationale: The participants are experienced software developers working on real open-source repositories where they have substantial prior contribution history (average 1,500 commits, 5 years experience per repo). They perform the full range of software development work: addressing bug reports, implementing features, writing code, submitting pull requests, responding to code reviews, and merging changes. This represents authentic software development work across the occupation's diverse activities, not a narrow coding task. The study measures their productivity doing their actual job across 246 real issues.
